query: Nissan leasing and PCP options available at Japanese dealers and how to choose between them
brand_topic_category: Value, offers, finance and total cost of ownership

request_params_without_api_key.q: Nissan leasing and PCP options available at Japanese dealers and how to choose between them

search_parameters.q: Nissan leasing and PCP options available at Japanese dealers and how to choose between them

text_blocks.snippet: At Japanese Nissan dealerships, drivers have access to two modern, flexible financing options structured around the future residual value of the car: Nissan ClickMobi (Nissan’s official all-inclusive car subscription/leasing service) and Nissan Big Value Credit (Nissan's version of a Personal Contract Purchase, known locally as Zanka Settei-gata Credit or "Zankure").
Both options defer a portion of the car's value to lower your monthly payments, but they handle ownership, vehicle taxes, and maintenance fees differently.
Comparison of Options Available at Japanese Dealers

table: Conditional sales contract / Deferral auto loan
Nissan Financial Services owns the car.
Dealer/Lender holds the title until the balloon is paid.

0 JPY initial cost options are standard.
Optional, but 10% or bonus-payment structures are common.
Car price, options, shaken inspection, annual auto taxes, and basic maintenance.
Car purchase price and options only. (Taxes and shaken are separate).
Optional; you can bundle or use your own.
Return the car, or upgrade to a new model.

text_blocks.snippet: ClickMobi functions as an all-inclusive subscription program designed for convenience. It bundles all hidden costs of driving in Japan into one predictable monthly invoice.

list.snippet: The Bundle Benefits: The monthly fee covers Japan's mandatory shaken (bi-annual vehicle safety inspection), annual automobile tax (Jidosha-zei), weight tax (Jungyo-zei), and routine dealer maintenance like oil changes.

The "1.5-Year" Loophole: While typical lease terms are 3 to 7 years, ClickMobi features a specialized 1.5-Year Plan. Under this structure (which uses a 3-year contract), you pay an upfront premium but can cancel and return the vehicle anytime after the 19th month without facing early termination penalties.

text_blocks.snippet: Option 2: Nissan Big Value Credit (PCP / Zankure)

This is a localized version of a Personal Contract Purchase (PCP). Nissan calculates a Guaranteed Minimum Future Value (GMFV) for the car at the start of your contract. You only finance the difference between the sticker price and that residual value.

list.snippet: How it Lowers Payments: Because the final "balloon" value is withheld from your active monthly amortization, your payments are significantly lower than a standard auto loan.

The Out-of-Pocket Expenses: Unlike ClickMobi, you are purchasing the vehicle via installments. Therefore, you must pay out-of-pocket for Japan's high annual automobile taxes and the heavy shaken inspection fees (which occur at the 3rd and 5th years).
Interest Considerations: Be aware that interest charges (手数料) are calculated based on the entire value of the car, including the deferred residual amount.

text_blocks.snippet: Whether choosing leasing or PCP at a Japanese dealer, strict rules apply to the vehicle's return state:

list.snippet: Mileage Limits: Contracts enforce a fixed allowance, usually capped at 1,000 km or 1,500 km per month. Exceeding this limit triggers a penalty penalty per kilometer (typically 5 to 10 JPY/km) upon vehicle handover.

Condition Rules: Dents, scratches, interior damage, or smoking inside the car will decrease its value below the guaranteed baseline. Drivers must pay for any restoration fees (Genzjo kaifuku) out-of-pocket when handing back the keys. (Note: ClickMobi covers up to 300,000 JPY of minor wear-and-tear damages automatically).

text_blocks.snippet: How to Choose the Best Option for You

list.snippet: You are an Expat with a Finite Stay: If your visa or work assignment in Japan is capped at 2 to 3 years, the lease structure protects you from the headache of privately selling a depreciated vehicle before leaving the country.

You Want Absolute Budget Predictability: If you want to avoid lump-sum surprises like the 50,000+ JPY annual tax bills in May or costly shaken inspections, leasing wraps everything into a single bill.

text_blocks.snippet: Choose Nissan Big Value Credit (PCP) if:

list.snippet: You Might Want to Own the Vehicle: If you fall in love with your Nissan Note e-POWER or Serena, PCP allows you to buy out the vehicle at the end of the term by paying or re-financing the balloon payment. ClickMobi leases are strictly return-only.

You Already Control Your Insurance and Maintenance: If you already maintain a high premium grade status (T等级) with a Japanese auto insurance provider or prefer using independent mechanics to save money, PCP gives you the flexibility to handle those elements outside of a dealer package.

serpapi_response.reconstructed_markdown:
At Japanese Nissan dealerships, drivers have access to two modern, flexible financing options structured around the future residual value of the car: [Nissan ClickMobi](https://ws.nissan.co.jp/) \(Nissan’s official all\-inclusive car subscription/leasing service\) and Nissan Big Value Credit \(Nissan's version of a Personal Contract Purchase, known locally as Zanka Settei\-gata Credit or "Zankure"\). [18] [8] [12] [11] [23]

Both options defer a portion of the car's value to lower your monthly payments, but they handle ownership, vehicle taxes, and maintenance fees differently. [11] [0] [15] [18]

### Comparison of Options Available at Japanese Dealers

| Feature | Nissan ClickMobi \(Leasing/Subscription\) | Nissan Big Value Credit \(PCP / Zankure\) |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Primary Nature | Long\-term operational lease / Subscription service | Conditional sales contract / Deferral auto loan |
| Ownership | Nissan Financial Services owns the car. | Dealer/Lender holds the title until the balloon is paid. |
| Contract Terms | 3, 5, or 7 years \(plus a flexible 1.5\-year trial plan\). | Usually fixed at 3, 4, or 5 years. |
| Down Payment | 0 JPY initial cost options are standard. | Optional, but 10% or bonus\-payment structures are common. |
| What is Included? | Car price, options, shaken inspection, annual auto taxes, and basic maintenance. | Car purchase price and options only. \(Taxes and shaken are separate\). |
| Optional Insurance | Optional; you can bundle or use your own. | Excluded; must be arranged independently. |
| End\-of\-Term Options | Return the car, or upgrade to a new model. | 1. Trade it in 2. Return it 3. Pay the balloon amount to keep it. |
[12] [18] [11] [8] [15] [16] [5] [10] [17] [6] [20] [2] [3] [1]

### Option 1: Nissan ClickMobi \(Car Leasing\)

ClickMobi functions as an all\-inclusive subscription program designed for convenience. It bundles all hidden costs of driving in Japan into one predictable monthly invoice. [16] [18] [20]

- The Bundle Benefits: The monthly fee covers Japan's mandatory shaken \(bi\-annual vehicle safety inspection\), annual automobile tax \(Jidosha\-zei\), weight tax \(Jungyo\-zei\), and routine dealer maintenance like oil changes.
- The "1.5\-Year" Loophole: While typical lease terms are 3 to 7 years, ClickMobi features a specialized 1.5\-Year Plan. Under this structure \(which uses a 3\-year contract\), you pay an upfront premium but can cancel and return the vehicle anytime after the 19th month without facing early termination penalties.

### Option 2: Nissan Big Value Credit \(PCP / Zankure\)

This is a localized version of a Personal Contract Purchase \(PCP\). Nissan calculates a Guaranteed Minimum Future Value \(GMFV\) for the car at the start of your contract. You only finance the difference between the sticker price and that residual value. [8] [5] [11] [4]

- How it Lowers Payments: Because the final "balloon" value is withheld from your active monthly amortization, your payments are significantly lower than a standard auto loan.
- The Out\-of\-Pocket Expenses: Unlike ClickMobi, you are purchasing the vehicle via installments. Therefore, you must pay out\-of\-pocket for Japan's high annual automobile taxes and the heavy shaken inspection fees \(which occur at the 3rd and 5th years\).
- Interest Considerations: Be aware that interest charges \(手数料\) are calculated based on the entire value of the car, including the deferred residual amount.

### Crucial Restrictions for Both Options

Whether choosing leasing or PCP at a Japanese dealer, strict rules apply to the vehicle's return state: [10] [18]

- Mileage Limits: Contracts enforce a fixed allowance, usually capped at 1,000 km or 1,500 km per month. Exceeding this limit triggers a penalty penalty per kilometer \(typically 5 to 10 JPY/km\) upon vehicle handover.
- Condition Rules: Dents, scratches, interior damage, or smoking inside the car will decrease its value below the guaranteed baseline. Drivers must pay for any restoration fees \(Genzjo kaifuku\) out\-of\-pocket when handing back the keys. \(Note: ClickMobi covers up to 300,000 JPY of minor wear\-and\-tear damages automatically\).

### How to Choose the Best Option for You
